rev: Process-art franchise that rotates through real artists and styles, with a gallery share at the end of each session.
Well-known chain that wraps fine-art technique in games, stories and songs; membership-based so commitment is expected.
Keeps a tight one-instructor-to-four-children ratio, so kids get genuine individual guidance.
Choice-based approach lets children pick their own projects; suits self-directed kids more than those wanting structure.
Small-group classes built around independence and reflection, drawing out each child's own personality.
Long-running structured curriculum spanning painting, handicrafts and comics; methodical rather than free-spirited.
Instructors work closely with each child regardless of ability, with branches sited in malls islandwide.
Established East-Coast studio that also accommodates children with learning differences; fairly far east.
Works from the belief that art is a learnable skill, with a relaxed Hougang space that doubles for parties.
Pasir Ris studio that pairs art-making with confidence and problem-solving; handy for far-east families.
Imaginative studio covering painting, sculpture and ceramics, so kids get genuine variety.
Korean-curriculum-inspired classes with an age-banded Little Explorers tier; single Orchard location.
Joo Chiat studio catering to ages 3-12 with a warm, kid-focused vibe popular with eastside parents.
Small-group taster of singing, keyboard, percussion, ukulele and violin before committing to one instrument.
Introduces both piano and violin to 4-6s, a solid way to test which instrument actually sticks.
Dual piano-and-violin early-years track, though it caps out around age five.
Tanglin studio with age-banded classes from babies to age seven, blending singing, instruments and movement.
Globally recognised play-based music-and-movement programme with several mall studios and a virtual option.
Combines music, dance and drama for preschoolers; good for active kids who learn best by moving.
Lays a structured musical foundation for under-fives, aimed at families serious about eventual instrument study.
